title: Photosynthesis and Cellular Respiration Cycle
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Step-through p5.js animation showing how photosynthesis and cellular respiration form a complementary cycle with traceable molecules and energy flow

learningObjectives: Explain how photosynthesis and cellular respiration form a complementary cycle
Trace the flow of energy and cycling of matter (CO2, O2, glucose, water) between producers and consumers
Compare the inputs and outputs of photosynthesis and cellular respiration
prerequisites: Basic understanding of atoms and molecules
Familiarity with chemical equations at a conceptual level

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Step-through animation with molecule counting and cycle visualization
keyQuestions: Why are photosynthesis and cellular respiration considered complementary processes?
Where does the energy enter the cycle and where does it leave?
Why does matter cycle but energy flows in one direction?
What would happen to atmospheric gases if photosynthesis suddenly stopped?
commonMisconceptions: Plants only do photosynthesis and animals only do respiration -- plants actually do both, but produce more O2 than they consume during the day
Photosynthesis and respiration cancel each other out -- energy is lost as heat at each step, requiring continuous solar input
Plants get their mass from the soil -- most plant mass comes from CO2 in the air, converted to glucose through photosynthesis
assessmentOpportunities: Have students trace a single carbon atom through the complete cycle as a narrative
Ask students to explain how a sealed terrarium sustains itself using the cycle model
Compare the molecule counts at each stage to verify conservation of matter
